key_insights.insight: Reflecting on mortality can help prioritize what truly matters in life.
key_insights.quote: When you get experiences like that just you have to be reminded sometimes that this is a game life is a game we're only on borrowed time right and you can never take yourself too seriously

key_insights.insight: It's important to understand your priorities in the current stage of life and make peace with the sacrifices you're making.
key_insights.quote: You've got to be honest with yourself and you've got to understand the situation. I know exactly where I'm at, what I'm thinking, what I'm doing, the sacrifices I'm making, what I could do better at, what I, you know, but I'm okay with it.

key_insights.insight: A deathbed moment reveals what truly mattered, stripping away the trivial concerns.
key_insights.quote: fuck that person criticizing my hair or my cut or this. None of that fucking ever mattered. All of that was trivial.

key_insights.insight: Being comfortable with yourself is a powerful state that unlocks effectiveness.
key_insights.quote: But when you get comfortable with yourself, it's a beautiful thing in life it really is you know when you wake up in the morning and you've always got to try for me and look good and feel good and be the best version of yourself you can be but you do get to a stage and that's actually when you become really powerful and effective

key_insights.insight: What matters to you in your youth will likely seem insignificant later in life.
key_insights.quote: I always say to people, you people, what matters to you now, you will look back on when you get to my age and go, I can't believe I even used to worry about stuff like that.

key_insights.insight: True passion is essential for maintaining a relentless work ethic.
key_insights.quote: it takes time to develop a passion for something and that that is the that's a key word, passion, because when we talk about being relentless and we talk about this work ethic, you can only have it if you love what you do.

actionable_advice.advice: Regularly reflect on your priorities and whether your actions align with them.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set aside time each week or month to assess your current focus and adjust your activities to better reflect what truly matters to you.

actionable_advice.advice: Make a conscious effort to become more comfortable with yourself.
actionable_advice.how_to: Practice self-acceptance, challenge negative self-talk, and focus on your strengths.

actionable_advice.advice: Identify activities you are truly passionate about.
actionable_advice.how_to: Experiment with different hobbies and careers until you find something that excites and motivates you.

memorable_stories: The guest recounts visiting his grandfather in his last days, prompting reflection on life's priorities.
The guest shares an anecdote about feeling self-conscious in his youth and how he's learned to accept himself.
The guest reflects on a Cambridge talk, contrasting his 'street smarts' with the intellectualism of the audience.
contrarian_claims: You cannot accurately plan your life based on how you might feel on your deathbed; you must act in the moment.
You don't need to be the best, just comfortable. The ones who know they're not the best are the ones everyone gravitates to.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 26 - Eddie Hearn: Don’t Forget What REALLY Matters
